title: Filibuster
weakest_section: 16:00-17:00 range with mostly functional setup jokes

jokes.text: I'm going to murder you a thousand times.
jokes.setup: April appears to be complimenting someone's appearance with 'You look lovely'
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with casual death threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - sweet/threatening juxtaposition delivered in her signature deadpan style

jokes.text: Someone's back from London / No, it's not the Queen / Someone's back from London / It's not Mr. Bean / It's not the royal baby / I think his name is Jeff
jokes.setup: Andy announces someone's return from London in song form
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd non-guesses including 'the royal baby' whose name he thinks is Jeff
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy randomness with the royal baby name being hilariously wrong, though a bit drawn out

jokes.text: Take a hike, Jerry. / It's Larry now. / Okay. Take a hike, Larry.
jokes.setup: April tells Jerry to leave using his wrong name
jokes.punchline: Jerry corrects her to Larry, she immediately dismisses him again with the 'correct' name
jokes.explanation: Efficient execution of long-running gag showing April's complete indifference to getting his name right

jokes.text: Last week, he tried to have me listed on the ballot as Leslie Buttface Hitler the Fourth.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains Jamm's attempts to sabotage her
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and offensive fake name he tried to use
jokes.explanation: Specific absurdity of 'Buttface Hitler the Fourth' is memorable and quotable

jokes.text: I'm so sorry. I just get excited when you like me.
jokes.setup: Ben seconds Leslie's motion in the council meeting
jokes.punchline: His awkward over-explanation of why he supported her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben awkwardness - his earnest need for approval creates cringe comedy

jokes.text: Give me those votes. / I don't have them on me.
jokes.setup: Leslie demands 'Give me those votes' with increasing intensity
jokes.punchline: Ben's literal interpretation that he doesn't physically possess votes
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective literal response to Leslie's metaphorical demand

jokes.text: I could be one of those dudes that kisses mannequins or whatever. But I like a woman in skates. A lot.
jokes.setup: Ben explains his roller skating fetish is 'innocuous'
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to people who kiss mannequins, then emphasizes his attraction 'A lot'
jokes.explanation: Ben's TMI confession gets uncomfortable in a character-appropriate way

jokes.text: Ann called. She wants her ugly outfit back.
jokes.setup: Classic setup format 'X called, they want Y back'
jokes.punchline: Calls Ann's outfit ugly using the familiar joke structure
jokes.explanation: Generic '90s joke format with no original twist - lazy writing for Tom

jokes.text: Leslie said it was a 'Come As You Were in the Nineties' party. I assumed it was the 1690s.
jokes.setup: April is dressed as a pilgrim for a '90s party
jokes.punchline: She interpreted '90s as 1690s instead of 1990s
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that's both logically absurd and perfectly April. The visual payoff is excellent.

jokes.text: Babe, if it was the 1690s, we'd all be mummies. / What do you think mummies are? / Skeletons?
jokes.setup: Andy points out logical flaw in April's costume choice
jokes.punchline: April thinks mummies are skeletons, showing complete historical ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the 1690s joke, showing April's confident ignorance about basic history

jokes.text: I actually just went. I had to pull my pants all the way to the ground like a toddler.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what happens when Chris has to pee in backwards pants
jokes.punchline: He describes the humiliating process of having to undress completely
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff to the backwards pants setup

jokes.text: Actually, we're not even playing the same sport. It's kind of like she's in the NBA, and I work in a muffler store next to the stadium.
jokes.setup: Tom says Nadia is out of his league
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor comparing their relationship to NBA vs. muffler store worker
jokes.explanation: Excellent extended metaphor that's both self-deprecating and specific to Tom's insecurities

jokes.text: I'm doing Doctors Without Borders, man. I'm going to Rwanda. / Exactly. That's a stupid vacation.
jokes.setup: Nadia explains she's doing humanitarian work in Rwanda
jokes.punchline: Tom dismisses life-saving medical work as a 'stupid vacation'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom selfishness - dismissing humanitarian work because it inconveniences him

jokes.text: Yet another professional Indian skee ball player? Right.
jokes.setup: Tom explains he didn't go pro in skee ball to avoid clichés
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically references the non-existent stereotype of Indian skee ball players
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about non-existent stereotypes, perfectly delivered with Tom's characteristic confidence

jokes.text: Hey, you know you're supposed to hit the deer, right?
jokes.setup: Ron is presumably missing all his shots at the hunting game
jokes.punchline: Donna's sarcastic observation about the basic premise of the game
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for Ron's hunting incompetence, functional but not special

jokes.text: this gun is lightweight, there's no sight, and we are far too close to these deer. Would they not smell us? I want my money back. How do I get my quarters?
jokes.setup: Ron struggles with the arcade hunting game
jokes.punchline: He critiques the game's realism like actual hunting, then pathetically asks for his money back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - applying serious hunting expertise to a children's game, then being petty about quarters

jokes.text: I am not a sore loser. It's just that I prefer to win. And when I don't, I get furious.
jokes.setup: Ron denies being a sore loser
jokes.punchline: Immediately describes exactly what a sore loser does
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction joke with perfect Ron delivery - completely lacks self-awareness

jokes.text: You know how you Americans say 'cool' when something's cool? Over in London, we say, 'That's brilliant.'
jokes.setup: Andy explains London slang to April
jokes.punchline: He explains the extremely common British slang 'brilliant' like it's exotic insider knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good Andy obliviousness - treating basic British vocabulary like secret cultural knowledge

jokes.text: I'm like Chuck Norris. Only instead of crushing bad guys' skulls, I'm crushing documents.
jokes.setup: Andy compares himself to Chuck Norris
jokes.punchline: The comparison is to paperwork instead of martial arts
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke showing Andy's attempts to make office work sound exciting

jokes.text: I'll karate punch your face with my tongue. / Prove it. / Hi-ya.
jokes.setup: Andy makes a ridiculous comparison about making out to karate
jokes.punchline: He actually makes a karate sound while kissing
jokes.explanation: Silly Andy moment that follows through on the absurd setup

jokes.text: He's got Monster, but not Automatic for the People. / Wow. / I know. It's like, what is this, a mid-'90s party?
jokes.setup: Ben complains about the DJ's REM album selection
jokes.punchline: He's upset the DJ has the wrong '90s album for his early '90s party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerdiness - caring intensely about the specific chronology of '90s music for his party

jokes.text: Are you dooking on my chest right now?
jokes.setup: Leslie announces she's filibustering
jokes.punchline: Jamm's crude and inappropriate metaphor for being surprised
jokes.explanation: Crude Jamm line that's character-appropriate but relies on shock value

jokes.text: She is filibustering on roller skates? / She's going to be strapped into those things for hours. / I swear to God, this is like a crazy sex fantasy for me.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes Leslie will filibuster in roller skates
jokes.punchline: He admits this is sexually exciting for him given his skate fetish
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes Ben's earlier confession pay off in an unexpected political context

jokes.text: Hey, you're supposed to be my lookout. / I thought it would be funnier to watch you get busted.
jokes.setup: Tom gets caught cheating at skee ball by his supposed lookout
jokes.punchline: Chris admits he deliberately let Tom get caught for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's mischievous side

jokes.text: I remember the first time I voted. I was in my bedroom, and I was five. I voted for Mildred, my stuffed pig. But my cat, Pancakes, won. She had more funding.
jokes.setup: Leslie shares a childhood voting memory during the filibuster
jokes.punchline: She treated a childhood game as a serious election, complete with campaign finance concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - applying political analysis to childhood pet elections shows her lifelong obsession with democracy

jokes.text: How long have I been talking? Three hours? / No, eight minutes.
jokes.setup: Leslie thinks she's been talking for hours
jokes.punchline: She's only been going for eight minutes
jokes.explanation: Classic time distortion joke showing how difficult the filibuster will be

jokes.text: This is literally the most exciting thing that I have ever seen.
jokes.setup: Ben watches Leslie's filibuster
jokes.punchline: He finds local government procedure genuinely thrilling
jokes.explanation: Good Ben nerd moment - his genuine excitement about mundane government procedure

jokes.text: Well, maybe the birthday party she's throwing for me right now.
jokes.setup: Ben says where else would he rather be than watching the filibuster
jokes.punchline: Points out he's missing his own birthday party
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about missing his own party for government procedure

jokes.text: I love Chinese crap. Lucy Liu, Nintendo, Gangnam Style, sushi, et cetera, et cetera.
jokes.setup: Jamm explains why he likes the gong
jokes.punchline: Lists random Asian things with no understanding of their different origins
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's ignorant lumping together of Asian cultures - uncomfortable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: What about, um, fifty bucks? / Yeah. Just don't tell my boss. / What are you doing, Morris? / Nothing, Mr. Bonderman.
jokes.setup: Tom bribes the arcade worker
jokes.punchline: The boss immediately appears and catches the transaction
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick timing of getting caught immediately after bribery

jokes.text: Every time you look up at the moon, I too, will be looking at a moon. Not the same moon, obviously. That's impossible.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to be romantic about looking at the same moon
jokes.punchline: He clarifies they can't literally see the same moon, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - ruins romantic gesture with literal interpretation. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: When I was a kid, I taught myself how to make out on this bear. I pretended it was Cockroach from The Cosby Show.
jokes.setup: Tom explains his attachment to the stuffed bear
jokes.punchline: Reveals he used it to practice kissing while fantasizing about a Cosby Show character
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable Tom TMI moment with specific '80s reference

jokes.text: That's so sweet, but you're basically offering me garbage.
jokes.setup: Tom offers consolation prizes
jokes.punchline: Nadia bluntly calls his backup gifts garbage
jokes.explanation: Efficient put-down that deflates Tom's romantic gesture

jokes.text: And if I know anything about Rwanda... And I don't. I bet it's full of rich guys who will buy her whatever she wants.
jokes.setup: Tom speculates about Rwanda
jokes.punchline: Admits complete ignorance then makes obviously wrong assumption
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his self-centered worldview and complete ignorance of global issues

jokes.text: I thought you were serious. / Come on, now. You know I don't give a [bleep].
jokes.setup: Ron suggests illegal nighttime hunting
jokes.punchline: When questioned about licenses, he reveals he doesn't care about laws
jokes.explanation: Typical Ron anti-government sentiment, though the bleep reduces impact

jokes.text: As Ronald Reagan would say, 'Well, Mommy, I believe voting rights are important.'
jokes.setup: Leslie quotes Ronald Reagan during her filibuster
jokes.punchline: The 'quote' is obviously made up and includes Reagan calling someone 'Mommy'
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's filibuster desperation - making up quotes and adding weird details like 'Mommy'

jokes.text: Well, speaking of heat, we should discuss America's white hot passion for voting rights, and the men and women who help protect them.
jokes.setup: Jamm sabotages the air conditioning
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately pivots the heat to a voting rights metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie improvisation - turning literal heat into political metaphor

jokes.text: Once she starts thinking about birthday cake, she's basically useless until she eats birthday cake.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions cake during her filibuster
jokes.punchline: Ben knows Leslie's specific food obsession patterns
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing Ben's intimate knowledge of Leslie's quirks

jokes.text: Democracy is not unlike a cake. It's layered, delicious, chocolate, and I want some.
jokes.setup: Leslie compares democracy to cake
jokes.punchline: The metaphor breaks down as she just wants actual cake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's cake fixation affecting her political speech

jokes.text: All we have is the right to vote. And the right to kill Indians without consequence. And I believe that half of that statement is still true today.
jokes.setup: Leslie quotes Pawnee's first mayor
jokes.punchline: The historical quote includes genocide, Leslie hopes only the voting part is still true
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that shows Parks & Rec addressing America's problematic past while maintaining Leslie's optimism

jokes.text: I was just here in this dumpster, because I jumped in.
jokes.setup: Andy is found in a dumpster
jokes.punchline: His explanation is just that he jumped in voluntarily with no reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - doing something inexplicable for no reason

jokes.text: We want someone from Eagleton to represent our interests on the new City Council. / Sir, I formally revoke our hug.
jokes.setup: The Eagletonians reveal they won't vote for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Ben formally revokes their previous hug
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ben formality applied to petty emotional response - very character-appropriate

jokes.text: We stand on the side of Rosa Parks. And Gandhi. And Team Aniston.
jokes.setup: Leslie lists civil rights heroes
jokes.punchline: Includes Jennifer Aniston from the Brad Pitt divorce as equal to civil rights icons
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke showing Leslie's pop culture obsessions bleeding into political speeches

jokes.text: Every day, someone comes up to me and says, 'I need your approval on this, Mr. Dwyer. I need your signature, Mr. Dwyer. That's not a toilet, Mr. Dwyer.'
jokes.setup: Andy describes workplace pressures
jokes.punchline: The final example is someone telling him something isn't a toilet
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - starts with normal workplace stress, ends with Andy's complete incompetence. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I am. But I'm like Chuck Norris now. Some old guy with a beard who used to be good at karate, but became a dumb has-been, and is scared and confused about his big London job.
jokes.setup: Andy maintains he's like Chuck Norris
jokes.punchline: Describes current Chuck Norris as a scared, confused has-been
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the metaphor while showing Andy's self-awareness about his struggles

jokes.text: Half the documents I get, I put right into the shredder because they're so boring.
jokes.setup: April explains how she handles her job
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to destroying important documents out of boredom
jokes.explanation: Perfect April casual destruction delivered in her signature deadpan style

jokes.text: Because you are awesome and everyone else sucks.
jokes.setup: April gives Andy encouragement
jokes.punchline: Her version of pep talk is that everyone else sucks
jokes.explanation: Efficiently delivered April wisdom - supportive but in her characteristically negative way

jokes.text: All I want is the promise of democracy. / You sure about that? / I want that marg. / I know you do.
jokes.setup: Leslie declares she wants democracy
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves and admits she wants the margarita
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's willpower breaking down under Jamm's temptation

jokes.text: Why are you covered in blood? / Don't worry. It's not human.
jokes.setup: Ron returns covered in blood
jokes.punchline: His reassurance that it's not human blood
jokes.explanation: Good dark Ron humor - the 'reassurance' is somehow more unsettling

jokes.text: Scientists say the emotions of love and hate are very close to each other. That's what I tell women.
jokes.setup: Ann asks how Tom gets women to date him
jokes.punchline: He uses fake psychology about love and hate being similar
jokes.explanation: Appropriately sleazy Tom pickup strategy that reveals his manipulative dating approach

jokes.text: I know conversations like this are hard for you because you still have feelings for me. But you'll land on your feet, kid.
jokes.setup: Tom thanks Ann for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Assumes she still has feelings for him and condescendingly calls her 'kid'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom narcissism and obliviousness to how relationships actually work

jokes.text: Did you... / Yes, I used cookies instead of bread.
jokes.setup: Andy made bologna sandwiches for April's trip
jokes.punchline: He used cookies as bread
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy gesture executed in characteristically weird way - cookies as bread is perfectly illogical

jokes.text: She's dancing around like she's a character in one of those Peanuts cartoons.
jokes.setup: Ben observes Leslie's desperate need for bathroom break
jokes.punchline: Compares her dancing to Peanuts characters
jokes.explanation: Solid visual metaphor for Leslie's predicament, though not particularly original

jokes.text: Hey, Knope, how you feeling over there? You need to go pee-pee? Pee-pee times? Make wee-wee?
jokes.setup: Jamm taunts Leslie about her bathroom needs
jokes.punchline: Uses infantilizing language to mock her predicament
jokes.explanation: Appropriately juvenile Jamm behavior but relies on cheap bathroom humor

jokes.text: She didn't have time to hug me, but she could do that?
jokes.setup: Leslie rushes to bathroom but stops to touch her hair
jokes.punchline: Ben's hurt reaction about hair being higher priority than hugging him
jokes.explanation: Good petty husband moment showing Ben's hurt feelings about priorities

jokes.text: Sorry, Ann. This is on you. You told us to make a memory. / There's your money back. / This is eight dollars.
jokes.setup: Tom and Nadia steal the bear following Ann's advice
jokes.punchline: Their 'refund' is only eight dollars for a forty-thousand-ticket bear
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with weak math joke about the refund amount

jokes.text: Now, that's what I call shooting. / That's a new record. / Hey, look at that. You finally made it into the top ten. / Now, you can enter your initials. / My initials are private.
jokes.setup: Ron finally succeeds at the hunting game
jokes.punchline: He refuses to enter his initials because they're 'private'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron privacy paranoia - treating arcade game leaderboard like government surveillance

jokes.text: I was peeing so freaking hard, it was like a Jacuzzi jet or... Hi, Leslie. / Hey. Ingrid. / I wasn't talking about the velocity of my pee.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes her bathroom experience
jokes.punchline: Gets caught mid-description and tries to backtrack awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Leslie gets caught in TMI moment, though bathroom humor is cheap

jokes.text: Not that forceful urination. The filibuster.
jokes.setup: Ingrid clarifies what she's thanking Leslie for
jokes.punchline: Has to specify she means the political act, not the bathroom experience
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to bathroom joke that clarifies the confusion but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Maybe I should put those roller skates back on. / Well, you must be exhausted. We don't have to go to the party. / Oh, we're not going to the party. We're going home. / Then, why would you need to put on roller skates? / Oh, yeah. Yeah, yeah, yeah. Yeah. We're just... Just. Skates.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggests putting roller skates back on
jokes.punchline: Ben realizes the sexual implication and they both get awkward about it
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Ben's fetish setup with appropriate married couple awkwardness

tmdb_overview: Leslie prepares a haunted house with Ben, Ann and Chris for Halloween on the day of the recall vote. Meanwhile, Tom fears the end of Rent-A-Swag and persuades Ron to help save his business.
insight: Cringe carries a middling episode: Leslie's marathon filibuster lands harder than the 72.8 score suggests.
